steps.title: Identify the structures
steps.content: Before the blank: “a fifteen-year-old Montgomery girl named Claudette Colvin was arrested for the same [BLANK]” is an independent clause. After the blank comes “According to some historians, Colvin's arrest led to Parks's action...” — another independent clause.

steps.title: Apply the independent clause separation rule
steps.content:
A period correctly separates two independent clauses, creating two complete sentences.

The other options fail: a comma (B) creates a comma splice, no punctuation (C) leaves a fused sentence, and “and according” (D) doesn't establish valid structure.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
